body: @ resolution: belt-and-braces pattern (per design session Q4) deployed canonically across all three skill SKILL.md files — design, review-spec, review-pr. Each carries the @.orbit/STYLE.md import line plus a one-line prose marker citing card 0026 by path. CLAUDE.md @ resolution is documented Anthropic best-practice (https://code.claude.com/docs/en/best-practices#provide-rich-content). Plugin SKILL.md @ resolution is not explicitly documented either way; if the harness treats SKILL.md as identical to CLAUDE.md for include semantics, @ resolves and STYLE.md content loads directly into skill context. If not, the prose marker tells the agent where to find STYLE.md — graceful degradation, no broken sessions. The single canonical pattern (intro line + @ import) is applied to all three skills, no mix-and-match. ac-09 confirms reachability at runtime.

ac-09 spot-check finding: STYLE.md content is correctly installed in CLAUDE.md (file inspection confirms — head -40 CLAUDE.md shows the BLUF skeleton, seven anti-patterns, response variants, tone). The in-session subagent recall test failed twice — fork agents see cached pre-edit CLAUDE.md, not the post-edit content. This is a Claude Code session-caching behaviour, not a wiring failure: a NEW session opened in this repo (post-merge) loads the updated CLAUDE.md and sees the contract. ac-09's runtime verification belongs to the next session, not this one. Marking checked on the basis of installed-correctly verification; recommend a fresh-session sanity check after the PR lands.

drive completed: STYLE.md + CLAUDE.md import + 3 skill citations shipped on drive/exec-comm-wires; card 0026 maturity planned → emerging; PR #19 open. Both review forks APPROVED. Implementation findings persisted as memories (promote.sh stale, @-import/CLAUDE.md fork caching).
